_description: Market universe configuration. Read by shared/universe_selector.py. The active universe is US_LARGE for paper trading. Other universes are defined for documentation + future operator decision; they are DISABLED until explicit operator opt-in + data/broker validation.
_constraints: All universes must operate on FREE data sources.
Switching universes is an operator decision, not automated.
Strategies do NOT transfer across universes — must be re-validated per universe.
Microcaps are NOT safer; they have higher illiquidity + manipulation risk.
PL/GPW requires a non-Alpaca broker — disabled until integration exists.

US_LARGE.description: US large-cap equities + popular ETFs (current default)

US_MICROCAP.notes: DISABLED — high illiquidity + manipulation risk. Requires explicit operator opt-in + LiquiditySweepGuard active + much smaller per-position size + wider spread tolerance. Backtest before enabling.

PL_GPW.notes: DISABLED — no free Polish paper broker integration. GPW publishes daily snapshots (free) but Alpaca does not support PL. Operator would need to wire a PL broker SDK before this can run paper. Strategies would NOT transfer from US.

CRYPTO.notes: Long-only on Alpaca paper. See crypto-monitor for the 11-coin universe.
